learnings.question: In Islamic theology, when a person's actions or supplications alter the actual occurrence of events in their life (Qada'), how does this outcome interact with Allah's preordained plan (Qadr)?
options: The change temporarily overrides and rewrites the preordained plan in Qadr.
The change permanently voids the original written plan, requiring a new destiny to be drafted.
The change and the decision to seek it were already known to Allah and are fully encompassed within Qadr.
The change operates completely outside of Qadr due to the exercise of human free will.
learnings.learning: While a person can alter the actual unfolding of events in their life (Qada') through actions and supplications, this change does not bypass divine destiny; both the action and the outcome were already known to Allah and are part of His complete plan (Qadr).
learnings.articleTitle: The Art of Qada’ and Qadr
